entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Sample Content for FI, HR and SD
entry.content:
@silke_jahr - did something change recently. 

when I try to import the data product, I get the following error: 

Couldn't import object 'SAP_SMPL_FI_SQL_ProductHierarchy [SAP_CONTENT\SAP_SMPL_FI_SQL_ProductHierarchy]' : We couldn’t save your object SAP_SMPL_FI_SQL_ProductHierarchy because it’s dependent on missing objects: SAP_SMPL_FI_DP_ProductHierarchy, SAP_SMPL_FI_DP_ProductHierarchy#DESCRIPTION, SAP_SMPL_FI_DP_ProductHierarchy#HIERARCHYID, SAP_SMPL_FI_DP_ProductHierarchy#LEVEL. Create or import the missing objects first, and then reimport your package.

 

but no idea where I should find the missing objects. Could you please check.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: BW Series – Getting Started (Part 2)
entry.content: @Manoj1849 We are planning to introduce a feature which is called multi-fact model, allowing you to include multiple fact sources into an Analytic Model. Concerning the second request, we will release a new feature in wave 02 (subject to change) enabling you to select dynamic default values.

entry.title: SAP Customer Data Platform B2C & B2B tenants
entry.content:
Hello, 

We have implemented SAP CDP for our B2C customers.

Now, we would like to do the same for B2B customers. 
I have seen in the following document that B2B use cases are available with CDP --> HERE

To activate B2B customers, should we use another CDP tenant or can we use the same database as for B2C, what's the recommended behavior? 

Thanks, 
Guillaume

SAP Customer Data Platform Customer Experience

entry.title: Re: Clear Facts & Classic Story BUG

entry.title: Re: Study Guide for SAP Certified Professional - Developer - SAP Commerce Cloud Certification Exam 2
entry.content: @Summitt12 , In the "Before we start" section, you should find links to various learning resources that provide comprehensive coverage of the key topics necessary for the certification exam. These resources offer detailed introductions to the subjects you'll need to master.

entry.title: Handling Production Order Change: Switching from Product A to Product B and Inventory Management
entry.content:
Scenario 1: Changing Production Order from Product A to Product B

Once a production order has been created and is actively processing on the production line for Product A, the organization may want to switch to producing Product B. However, most of the Bill of Materials (BOM) for both products are the same.

How can we handle this kind of scenario effectively? What ideas or previous methods have been used in similar situations?

Scenario 2: Managing Inventory in the Above Scenario

If the scenario described above (switching production from Product A to Product B) is feasible, how can we effectively manage the inventory to ensure the correct materials are used and any unused materials are properly handled?

entry.title: Re: Help with reporting layout in SAP Datasphere/ Analytics Cloud/ Web Intelligence
entry.content: Looking at the data in the table, all rows contain an 'Others' value - so the filtered result with no data is kind of expected.

entry.title: Re: How can get the two last record of a internal table a...
entry.content:
Hi juan_surosThe system generate error when the table have a record  this code:

DATA: lt_from TYPE stringtab.
  DATA: lt_to TYPE stringtab.
*  lt_from = VALUE #( ( |hic| ) ( |hik| ) ( |hok| ) ).
  lt_from = VALUE #( ( |hic| ) ).
  APPEND LINES OF lt_from FROM lines( lt_from ) - 1
                          TO lines( lt_from )
                          TO lt_to.

entry.title: You want to terminate a system which is no longer needed
entry.content:
How do I terminate a Test tenant ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: You want to terminate a system which is no longer needed
entry.content:
I you want to terminate a Test Tenant navigate to the:

Workcenter:Service Control center

View Systems select the Test system and click terminate system

 

Note if you need to terminate a Productive Tenant please follow the below process and or KBA :

https://me.sap.com/notes/2741988

 

NOTE:In order to terminate your existing production tenant. Please create a Case from your test tenant with the details like, Production tenant ID, Production Tenant URL and Reason for the termination.

I have gone through  SALV_TEST_HYPE_REFRESH where there are coding like: building handler with salv_de_hyperlink_handle or set_cell_type( if_salv_c_cell_type=>link ) and set_hyperlink_entry() .. but those work on-screen and handling navigation on GUI screen. I do not see anything for excel file download. Please advise...

Thanks Ram
I did try with the set_cell_type( if_salv_c_cell_type=>link ) but it has no effect.

entry.title: Re: RAP OData v4 Web-API metadata set nullable option
entry.content: @TuncayKaraca This works for standard CDS entities. Regarding custom CDS entities I figured out that the annotation and the definition of a NullValueIndicator field of type boole_d is sufficient to remove nullable="false" there. For abstract entities this does not seem to hold true, do you know how i can suppress nullable="false" there?

Sumit

entry.title: Job Requisition Created Through Position Org Chart does not show association with Position
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have once scenario where Recruiter have created new Job Requisition via position org chart almost a month back. As of today, when Recruiter access same position through org chat, Job Requisition created previously is not showing association with that position and create new Job Requisition option is showing for that position under position org chart.

Were as when we check the Job Requisition under Requisition tab, previously created Job requisition is lag and showing associations to that position. Any idea what could be the cause?

entry.title: Re: Error loading webview loading Sap Business Application Studio

entry.title: Sopra Steria Achieves SAP® DevOps Certification for Operations Partners
entry.content:
Who We Are

Sopra Steria is a prominent player in the European tech sector, specializing in providing managed services in Spain and Europe. The SAP DevOps certification ensures that we are a recognized partner by SAP, capable of offering advanced solutions to automate the daily tasks of SAP system operations.

Our Journey to Certification

We began our journey by addressing a specific client need to configure and provision over 50 SAP systems at cloud in a very short time (complete SAP systems, SAP S/4HANA ASCS/PAS, HANA DB, SAP WebDisp.).

This initial challenge drove us to automate the most requested SAP BASIS tasks, such as access management (ABAP Stack, SAP BTP, SAP SuccessFactors), opening SAP connections (AWS/Azure/Rise), and other essential tasks to maintain system operability.

Over time, we have developed a comprehensive catalog of automated services available on various SAP hyperscalers. These services can be requested through ITSM tools like ServiceNow or executed directly on-demand by our teams, providing flexibility and efficiency to our clients.

Currently, we are expanding our services to include the automation of functional and technical deployments across different environments, thus improving the software delivery cycle. This evolution allows us to offer faster, more reliable solutions tailored to our clients’ changing needs.

Benefits of SAP DevOps Certification for Our Clients

Our recent SAP DevOps certification offers our clients:

  • Assured Quality: Services that meet the highest industry standards, delivering top-class solutions.
  • Agility and Efficiency: Fast and adaptable solutions to meet their needs.
  • Regulatory Compliance: Adherence to industry best practices established by SAP.
  • Technical Expertise: Support from experts in SAP implementation and management.
  • Continuous Innovation: Incorporation of the latest technologies and improvements.

With this certification, our clients enjoy more agile and efficient operations, with the peace of mind that they are backed by the highest industry standards. This not only enhances productivity and reduces costs but also allows businesses to focus on what truly matters.

Our Approach to DevOps

At Sopra Steria, we adopt a collaborative and agile approach to DevOps. We work closely with our clients to understand their needs and objectives, designing solutions that enable them to achieve their business goals more quickly and effectively.

Conclusion

We are proud of this achievement and grateful to our clients and team for their continuous support. We will continue striving to offer innovative and high-quality solutions that drive our clients’ success.

Financial accounting - Asset accounting - Integration with General ledger accounting - Integrated Transactions: Alternative Doc. Type for Acctg-Princpl-Spec. Docs.

Regards,

Sumesh Gupta

entry.title: Re: Error loading webview loading Sap Business Application Studio
entry.content: just need to add cloud.sap on trusted list of the antivirus's antibanner similar to what @luizems said

entry.title: SuccessFactors AI - Job Description Enhancement (Set up and hints)
entry.content:
Job Description Enhancement is one of our powerful AI function for SuccessFactors Recruiting Management.

It helps a recruiter or hiring manager to enhance a generic job description using generative AI to better reflect their specific hiring needs.

Setting up this function is very simple. As soon as your company has licensed some AI Units the option to activate the function is available in your SuccessFactors System.

It's more or less a 3 step approach (guide)

1. Activate the function via "AI Administration" Page

 

 

2. define fields that should be used for the enhancement (e.g. department name, company name,e tc.) via MDF Object Recruiting AI Enhanced Job Description

I would recommend to check the object against your configuration and add missing fields to improve your final result. 

e.g. if the Job Description Enhancement functions knows what the professional level should be or for what department the job is foreseen it will make the final result better.

3. User permission (RBP)  - make the function available to your recruiters

 

After finishing the steps your recruiters (or who ever has permissions) are able to use the AI tool für Job Description Generation.

 

Some key learnings:

1. Language

Job Description Enhancement (Gen AI) is available in different languages. Your POSTING LANGUAGE defined the AI language.

Define the posting languages on your job description. Depending on the selected language for the posting the AI tool will give your the result in the selected language.

Available languages:  3396784 - SuccessFactors Generative AI Multilingual Creation - SAP for Me

2. Job Profiles and Job Profile Builder

Using Job Profiles and a strong maintained JPB is NOT necessary (also TIH is not needed).

JPB would help you in getting good results and would help your recruiting team with predefined posting texts, skills, competencies etc. but you can use the AI functionality without it.

3. Result

The result of the Job Description Enhancement depends on your input and the quality of your input.

Let us assume you run the AI Enhancer WITHOUT any information on the job description / summary fields. In this case the result would not be very sufficient - hope this is clear. The tool will use the information from the job description (based on the MDF) and tries to create a description. 

Possible result:

 

Now give a little bit more instruction to the description and see the AI magic:

And what happens if the recruiter creates a nearly perfect description from the beginning? In this case the AI can not be so much better. The result would be nearly the same. Perhaps some small adjustments in texts/wordings are done but no bigger changes in the overall context.

In such cases - congrats to the recruiters because they have done a very well job 😉

 

 

What are your learnings? Something to share with me or others?

entry.title: What is SAP Asset & Service Management?
entry.content:
What is SAP Asset & Service Management?

SAP Asset & Service Management is a comprehensive, AI-powered suite for managing the entire asset lifecycle while driving operational excellence. This blog explores how it is setting new benchmarks in asset performance and reliability—and yes, there’s a demo video to showcase its impact.

Across asset-intensive industries, SAP Asset & Service Management delivers intelligent solutions to solve real-world challenges and unlock value. From oil, gas, & energy to utilities, travel/transportation, mining, manufacturing, aerospace & defense, and beyond, SAP is the trusted partner for organizations aiming to stay ahead.

Our solution combines advanced AI and automation with practical tools to optimize every aspect of asset operations—from strategic planning and predictive maintenance to efficient scheduling and seamless execution. With SAP, businesses don’t just manage assets; they drive operational excellence, improve sustainability, and continuously improve with closed feedback loops.

Watch this quick demo video to see SAP in action—empowering organizations to help their assets run better:

Reflecting high customer satisfaction in the enterprise asset management (EAM) market, IDC has awarded SAP the 2024 SaaS EAM Customer Satisfaction Award. This recognition underscores SAP’s dedication to delivering innovative solutions that empower organizations to achieve operational excellence and long-term sustainability.

Additionally, SAP has been recognized as a Leader in both the IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Field Service Management Applications 2023-2024 Vendor Assessment (doc #US49989523, December 2023) and IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Service Life-Cycle Management Platforms 2023-2024 Vendor Assessment (doc #US49989623, October 2023). Learn more about what analysts are saying about Asset & Service Management from SAP.

Key Strengths of SAP Asset & Service Management

Integral Part of SAP's ERP Suite

SAP Asset & Service Management is deeply embedded in the core ERP suite, offering advanced capabilities for Asset Performance Management and Field Service Management. This embedded approach ensures that complex asset and service requirements are met within a unified business environment.

SAP Asset & Service Management

AI-Infused Asset Lifecycle Management

SAP Business AI transforms the asset lifecycle, enabling predictive failure analysis, optimized scheduling, and actionable insights through tools like SAP’s Co-Pilot, Joule. These innovations simplify processes, enhance decision-making, and automate tasks, helping organizations proactively address challenges and adapt to changing demands.

AI-infused Asset & Service Management


A Unified Asset Data Thread

A digital thread of asset data ensures seamless integration of technical and operational information, creating a single source of truth for asset management. By unifying data across systems, this approach enhances governance, ensures accuracy, and enables organizations to make informed decisions while aligning with industry standards.

Integrate Asset & Service Management accross the enterprise

Closed-Loop Asset Management

A closed-loop asset management approach integrates asset data, risk assessments, maintenance strategies, and execution insights into a continuous improvement cycle. By leveraging IoT insights, advanced analytics, and automated workflows, it optimizes reliability, minimizes downtime, and enhances safety while aligning with ESG goals. The result is smarter, more sustainable asset performance with ongoing improvements.

Closed Loop Asset Lifecycle Management

Asset Reliability Engineering & Risk Management

Organizations can enhance reliability and manage risks at scale with a solution that identifies critical assets, prioritizes risk, and supports systematic maintenance strategies. This approach enables improved ESG outcomes and minimizes disruptions through smarter asset management.

Asset Reliability Engineering & Risk Management

Maintenance Planning and Optimization

Dynamic planning tools from SAP balance maintenance demands with available resources, making them ideal for Maintenance, Repair, and Overhaul (MRO) activities. Scenario-based simulations enable organizations to optimize schedules, reduce costs, and ensure timely maintenance while minimizing downtime.

Maintenance Planning and Optimization

EAM and FSM Convergence

SAP bridges the gap between Enterprise Asset Management (EAM) and Field Service Management (FSM), delivering a holistic solution for managing both internal and customer assets. This unified approach enhances operational efficiency and optimizes equipment performance across the board.

EAM and FSM Convergence

Sustainable and Safe Operations

Embedded sustainability and safety features ensure maintenance practices are compliant, safe, and aligned with regulatory goals. These capabilities span risk management, regulatory and permit compliance, and optimizing resource assignments through predictive routing to reduce travel times and CO₂ emissions. They also help prevent environmentally damaging asset failures while extending the useful life of assets and supporting long-term operational sustainability.

Sustainability and Compliance as Core Principles

Mobile Work Management

According to the US Bureau of Labor Statistics, job openings in installation, maintenance, and repair occupations are projected to grow significantly in the coming years. Coupled with a shortage of skilled labour, this makes mobile work management tools more critical than ever. SAP's mobile work management solutions enable field technicians to access work orders, asset and customer data, and instructions in real time—whether online or offline. With connected data and intuitive workflows, mobile workers are empowered to perform at their best.

Mobile Work Management

Comprehensive Health Monitoring and Analytics

With agile asset health monitoring and advanced analytics, SAP Asset Performance Management enables organizations to proactively manage asset health, uncover trends, and address issues before they escalate. By enhancing reliability, reducing unplanned downtime, and extending asset lifecycles, it delivers smarter, data-driven insights that optimize asset performance. 

Comprehensive Health Monitoring and Analytics

Collaborative Ecosystem and Business Networks

Collaboration is at the heart of effective asset management. An adaptable, extensible platform allows stakeholders to seamlessly share asset data and drive collaborative work management processes tailored to industry needs. Furthermore, partner solutions like Cumulocity IoT, Locana, and AsInt further enhance capabilities, enabling organizations to achieve mutual goals in asset performance and operational excellence.

SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration

The true measure of SAP Asset & Service Management’s impact is reflected in our customers' success. Discover how companies are revolutionizing their asset/service management practices and achieving operational excellence with SAP:

  • Equinor Boosts Asset Performance with Condition-Based Maintenance Enabled by SAP Asset Performance Management
  • Freeport-McMoRan: Using Mobile Apps to Make Asset Maintenance Faster, Safer, and More Efficient
  • Patterson Dental: How Does Integrated Field Service Management Help Save Time and Cut Costs?
  • Swiss Federal Railways Speeds Ahead with SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration

Organizations worldwide are unlocking the potential of SAP’s asset management solutions, improving reliability, boosting operational efficiency, and achieving sustainability goals—all with smarter, AI-powered tools designed for the future.

Ready to transform your asset and service management? Visit www.sap.com/eam to explore solutions, or register for our upcoming webinars for the latest insights.

Thoughts or questions? Feel free to share any feedback in a comment. For more information about SAP Asset & Service Management solutions, visit www.sap.com/eam. I also encourage you to register for our upcoming webinars for the most up to date information by registering here.

entry.title: Calculations in a specific planning version
entry.content:
Good morning,
I am creating a story where I am copying data from the actual version to the planning version. When the data is copied to planning, it increases the gross salary calculation based on parameters. These parameters are associated with January 2024, and in the Data Action, the calculation is also linked to January 2024.

I have one button for copying and another button for the calculation. However, when I execute the calculation, it does not work. Could you assist me in resolving this issue?

I will send screenshots to provide more context to the question.

The purpose of the table is that in January 2025, a 50% increase is applied to "Rec1," meaning it should have 1,200 euros plus 50%.

Thanks in advance,

Ganjos

entry.title: Landed Costs with Fixed Assets shows error
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am using SAP Business One 10.0 version for SAP HANA (10.00.270) SP 2411. While creating Landed Costs document with fixed assets, I have faced with this error: "[Landed Costs - Rows - Duty Rate][line: 10] , 'No matching records found 'GL Accounts' (OACT) (ODBC -2028)' [Message 131-183]". I attached screencast of issue. What is the duty rate in the rows? There is no this column. 

SAP Business One, version for SAP HANA

Regards,

MHK

entry.title: A year of Collaboration and Innovation: SAP Open Source 2024 Report
entry.content:
As we step into 2025, it’s the perfect time to look back and share the SAP Open Source 2024 report with you. This report reflects on the key accomplishments that have shaped SAP's open-source journey in the past year, with a special focus on the people behind the projects and their collaborations with foundations and communities. By sharing many quotes about motivations, relationships, and connections, we aim to provide a more personal and relatable look at SAP’s open-source journey. 

Let me share highlights from the report and hope it gives you insight into what has been happening at SAP, while celebrating the commitment and great contributions of individuals and teams both within and outside of SAP. 

People-driven open source 

Compared to contributors to SAP-led projects, more than 54% of SAP colleagues contributed to community-led projects in 2024 as. Götz Lindenmaier, lead maintainer of the OpenJDK 17 updates at SAP, shared his motivation for contributing: "Our long-standing engagement in the OpenJDK community results in a good reputation that allows us to solve problems in collaboration with outstanding OpenJDK developers and to affect decisions about new features." For Götz and many others, it's joint problem-solving, good citizenship, and co-innovation that drive their support for open source projects.  

Positive feedback from the community

The report features positive feedback from external contributors regarding SAP-initiated open-source projects. You'll also find an overview of these projects, including the Apeiro Reference Architecture project. Key components of ApeiroRA, such as Gardener, OCM, and OpenMFP, offer a neutral and open interoperability approach based on existing, well-accepted cloud-native ecosystem standards. 

Looking ahead to 2025 

In 2025, we will see the formation of the NeoNephos Foundation, a collaborative endeavor with the Linux Foundation Europe and other organizations. This initiative will establish neutral community governance for SAP’s open-source projects under the Apeiro Reference Architecture project and complementary projects from the IPCEI-CIS initiative. Peter Giese, Head of the SAP Open Source Program Office and Linux Foundation Advisory Board member, states, "The new NeoNephos Foundation is a testament to SAP’s commitment to open-source collaboration." 

Commitment to foundations 

A highlight of 2024 was our involvement as a founding member of the OpenSearch Software Foundation. This initiative aims to foster the development of OpenSearch, an open-source search and analytics suite. Michael Ameling, Executive Vice President and Chief Product Officer of the Business Technology Platform at SAP, explains the strategic viewpoint behind this collaboration: "Being a founding member of the new OpenSearch Software Foundation is a significant milestone for our strategic involvement and for our unified, business-centric, and open SAP Business Technology Platform." 

Community engagement and more 

The report also covers SAP’s community engagement, our open-source champions, and a chapter about openness in AI, offering a glimpse into topics like GitHub Copilot applied to open source, open Large Language Models, and AI-generated code contributions. 

 

This report demonstrates that open source is driven by people committed to openness and collaboration. It shows that SAP aligns with the principles shared in the SAP Open Source Manifesto, published in 2024. Thank you to everyone who added value to this report, actively engaged in communities and projects, or helped manage open-source efforts to ensure ease of use, security, and compliance. 

______________________________________________________________________________________ 

**Join us in shaping the future — one contribution at a time.** 

*Note: If you're interested in learning more about our open-source journey, we invite you to explore our resources, including blog posts, the SAP Open Source Community page, and GitHub repositories.

entry.title: Re: Internal Error in MDG CR form
entry.content: Problem solved. This problem occured because one of the material ledgers was not active.

entry.title: Re: GET THE PAST 12 MONTHS
entry.content:
parameter: lv_date type sy-datum.


data : lv_date1 like sy-datum,
       lv_date2 like sy-datum.

CALL FUNCTION 'MONTH_PLUS_DETERMINE'
  EXPORTING
    months        = '-12'
    olddate       = lv_date
 IMPORTING
   NEWDATE       = lv_date1.
          .


CALL FUNCTION 'MONTH_PLUS_DETERMINE'
  EXPORTING
    months        = '+11'
    olddate       = lv_date1
 IMPORTING
   NEWDATE        = lv_date2.


write: lv_date1,lv_date2.

entry.title: Create 2 goal sections on PM Form from samegoal plan, but another goal category is shown
entry.content:
Hi all, 

Current situation: 

  • 1 goal section on PM Form linked to a goal plan that has 2 goal categories
  • The goal sections shows all the goals of the 2 goal categories

Desired situation: 

  • 2 goal sections on PM Form linked to a goal plan that has 2 goal categories
  • The first section, only shows the goals of the first  goal category
  • The second section, only shows the goals of the second goal categoy

So, my question is: is it possible to create 2 goal sections on the PM form that are linked to the SAME goal plan, but every sections displays another goal category defined in the goal plan? 

I see it's possible to configure "selective addition of goals". This means that you can define in the PM XML template that a particular goal category should be shown. I have the feeling it's not possible to define per goal section which category should be shown as it's about the same goal plan? 

Thx!

Kind regards, 

Ellen

entry.title: Re: Handling Production Order Change: Switching from Prod...
entry.content:
Hello @sriram_007 

In my opinion the easiest way to handle the switch is to TECO the production order for product A and to create a new production order for product B. This way all component reservations of the 1st order will be closed and replaced with the reservation of the 2nd order. Thus you won't have any issues handling components inventory.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
@jbetanpr and @graziela 

Hello, did you get the 2025 WA PFML rates?  I haven't seen them but thought to double check with anybody as I might have missed something.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Payment Voucher and Payment Workflow
entry.content:
Hi,

Do you mean you will post the outgoing payments manually?

Through App Post Outgoing Payments, you can manually conduct the payments to the suppliers.

But I don't think workflow can be set for this outgoing payment app.

Kind Regards,

Lucy

Could you please confirm if there is any glitch or update in SAP Build Apps?

Regards,

Savan Patel

9033993882

entry.title: Time Valuation Results - Project Controlling Object
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are implementing EC, EC Payroll and Time Tracking for a public sector client. We have configured PSM in Employee Central and activated the Project Controlling Object (WBS). We would like to use this in Time Valuation to split time recordings in time valuation results like the system does standard with Cost Centre. Is there any way of doing this or on the road map?

Thanks!

Best,
Nicolai

Boris

entry.title: Re: Query Pending Workflow Details via entity TodoV2 API
entry.content:
Hi,

from API perspective "getWorkflowPendingData" could help.

This shows more or less the things you can see on the UI.

Depending on your use case you can also think about to use iframes to show the workflow request/details in a iframe.

entry.title: Re: Change Package Transport layer - Public Cloud
entry.content: Hi @VK2025, Unfortunately, no. There is a workaround: Move to git, delete from server and retrive again from git. The problem is that not all objects can be moved to github, so we were afraid to lose something important. Furthermore, be aware that bsps alredy deployed cannot be moved to a new package with different transport layer, so you will need to redeploy them.

entry.title: Re: SDK Journal Entry Update Invalid cash flow
entry.content: Hi nadav.caridi2,

entry.title: Re: SDK Journal Entry Update Invalid cash flow
entry.content:
Hi Nadav.caridi2,

I'm experiencing the same issue. Were you able to find a solution?

I'm currently working with SAP Business One 10.0 for SAP HANA (10.00.230) SP 2311 (64-bit).

entry.title: Re: Handling Production Order Change: Switching from Prod...
entry.content:
Dear @Dominik,

Thank you for your valuable comment.

In the case of TECO, the system will treat the order as completed, and the materials will be considered as consumed for the respective order.

However, the physical stock will still remain in the WIP location, correct?

entry.title: Re: Change Package Transport layer - Public Cloud
entry.content: Hi @VK2025, Unfortunately, no 😞 There is a workaround: Move to git, delete from server and retrive again from git. The problem is that not all objects can be moved to github, so we were afraid to lose something important. Furthermore, be aware that bsps alredy deployed cannot be moved to a new package with different transport layer, so you will need to redeploy them. For other objects, move 1 by 1.

entry.title: Re: Alternative for Advanced Financial Closing
entry.content:
Hi @max_mueller007 

SAP Advanced Financial Closing is SAP's strategic solution for the financial close and is available only on BTP. Being a could solution, the AFC is updated with new functionalities on a regular basis and you would benefit from all of them directly, see for example the What's New on SAP Help.

Regarding AFC pricing, please get in touch with your SAP sales representative to check options based on your prospective usage.

Kind regards

Jana

Also, if manual payment is made, how to get Payment Voucher?

entry.title: Re: CIM Error in S4 : Your tenant is not trusted
entry.content: Can you please check that you have done the final step « Authentication Restriction » in https://help.sap.com/docs/CENTRAL_INVOICE_MANAGEMENT/7648a91c50d84e38ace80037f82fefae/523af52f789a4f2aa9aa1ce2af5e461d.html?locale=en-US? IT not done it should fail for all users though. If done, then you should create a support request ticket (you may have already done so indeed).

But it works for Journal documents posted through T code like FB60.

Could you please help .

Regards

Dipin

entry.title: Re: My CAP Projects Toolbox
entry.content:
Hi @qmacro , thanks a lot for the valuable feedback!

Having a look at what's under the hood always gives one a better understanding of what is really going on indeed. I always like to know what's happening. 🙂 

Thanks a lot again for clarifying DJ!

Dries

entry.title: Re: Updating the ShortText of an Inspection Lot
entry.content:
Hello there

It seems you've got a few things mixed up: the inspection lot short text has TDID = 'QALS'. 'QAVE' on the other hand is for the Usage Decision long text. Also, as @raymond_giuseppi correctly pointed out, SAVE_TEXT will save the long text. But you should still be able to use it, since the first 40 (?) characters of the long text are automatically saved into QALS-KTEXTLOS. Lastly, only concatenate MANDT and PRUELFOS into TDNAME (i.e. without 'L').

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Excel Download with hyperlinks
entry.content:
With SALV, there is no built-in feature to define a HTTP hyperlink cell: with an ALV hotspot cell, you also need ABAP code to open the Web page via an event handler, TO_XML can't know that and can never generate a hyperlink cell in the Excel file. Also, as far as I know, TO_XML never interprets cell contents as formulas, they are formatted as raw texts or numbers.

NB: don't use the method "cl_alv_table_create=>create_dynamic_table" which is not released, also it's based on GENERATE SUBROUTINE POOL, which is slow and does a runtime error after 36 repeated calls, instead use the Z alternative here (based on released RTTS, which is fast and doesn't fail): Alternative to CL_ALV_TABLE_CREATE=>CREATE_DYNAMIC... - SAP Community.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Code - Create Application Logic with Joule
entry.content:
Hi @samshenrogers ,

Try these steps to resolve the issue:

  1. Refresh or Restart the Session:
    Log out of SAP Joule and log back in. Clear your browser cache and cookies, then refresh the page.

  2. Check Browser Compatibility:
    Use a supported browser like Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge (make sure it’s updated). If the issue persists, try switching to another browser.

  3. Test Another Entity:
    Open a different entity in the Graphical Modeler to see if the problem is specific to the Purchases entity or impacts all entities.

If the issue persists after trying these steps, let me know.

For a complete guide on using SAP Joule, I’ve created an end-to-end roadmap that covers:

  • Assigning Roles and Building Apps Using Boosters in SAP BTP.
  • Creating a Dev Space and Projects in SAP Joule.
  • Building CAP Applications for SAP EWM Integration with Joule Prompt.
  • Enhancing CAP Applications by Adding New Entities and Back-End Logic.
  • Creating and Deploying UI Applications Using the BAS Wizard in SAP Joule.

You can refer to my detailed blog for more insights:
The Future of SAP is Here: Meet SAP Joule

 

Thanks & Regards,

Satyasri

entry.title: Re: Configure SAP Enterprise Messaging for SAP S/4HANA On-Premise via Service Key
entry.content: The reported error isn't self-explanatory?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Functionality for EDI Processing: Setup and Testing of API for Inbound Order Creatio
entry.content:
Excelent blog @M_Kalyabin . This is a route that we are also taking, moving away from IDOC based EDI to Web Services based EDI. It has been an hard route since SAP documentation is still a bit scarse and we can't find a lot infomration on the full end-to-end process.

Have you had to add standard sales order fields to the Sales Order/Customer Return - Confirm Processing (B2B) -OrderRequest_In SOAP Web Service ?

For example, the standard Shipping Conditon field is not in the Sales Order/Customer Return - Confirm Processing (B2B) -OrderRequest_In SOAP Web Service but it is on Sales Order (A2X)-Sales Order Header POST /A_Order Odata Web Service and also on Sales Order (A2A) -SalesOrderBulkRequest_In SOAP Web Service .

entry.title: Re: Handling Production Order Change: Switching from Prod...
entry.content:
Hello @sriram_007 

TECO will not consume any components. Whatever has been already consumed before TECO will remain consumed, i.e. if some quantity of product A has already been delivered and components have been consumed for that, it will remain as such during TECO. So whatever stock has not been consumed for the 1st production order will remain at the work center and the stock can be used for the 2nd order.

Cheers

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: POST Requests Being Converted to GET in SAP BTP Cloud...
entry.content: Thank you, this was it in my case! I did not specify any protocol, and after adding https://, the request got through as POST

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps as Business Solution for SAP Build Work Zone - Part 2
entry.content:
Hello @matheusog 

are you following the new CDM based approach which is described in this blog or the former approach, which is more based on destinations per app-host and XSUAA which is described in https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/designing-ui5-apps-for-sap-launchpad-service-part-4/ba-p/13724339 

The 2nd screenshot of your MTA looks like the old destination based approach. The usage of a business service via service broker is partially shown in this post. My new blog series does not cover this yet.

Is your problem related to integrate the UI resources or how to connect an OData service directly?
For the routing of the OData service please do not use "destination" but "service" and provide the sap.cloud.service tag.
For integration of UI resources, you should not have any routings in your xs-app.json at all. The platform has to provide these mappings. It is important that the using app properly declares the dependencies to the library and that the business service provides the Id of its own app-host in its service credentials. In order to achieve this, you must bind the app-host to the broker app.

If it still does not work, please provide the URLs of the failing requests.

Best regards
Matthias

entry.title: Re: Translation of SAP Analytics Cloud content in multiple language Add-On
entry.content: Great post, but what about translation process in SAC Live Models?

entry.title: modifying the defaut email address in s4 output parameter determination
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to know if it is possible in 'output parameter determination' to modify the settings so that the default email address of the recipient of a billing document is the 'clerk internet address' specified at the company code level of the BP record and not the generic email address.

Thanks

Erwan

entry.title: Re: Story usage by user
entry.content:
Hi @alexander_john_berlin,

Yes, it is possible to see how much time each user spends on a specific story in SAP Analytics Cloud.

To do this, follow these steps:

  1. Go to System > Overview > Home tab.
  2. Locate and open the story you want to analyze.
  3. Click on Performance Analysis.

Here, you can check detailed information about which users have accessed the story and how much time they spent on it.

Hope this helps!

If you find this useful, please upvote.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: OOABAP
entry.content:
Sequence of Constructor Calls
https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/acquire-core-abap-skills/implementing-inheritance_bfdb59f7-0f99-48b9-b019-a7b766830ecc

entry.title: Re: Second ledger HGB
entry.content: Hello Javier, may I can ask a question? Could you pls. specify "adapt IFRS principles to your DEAP"? What needs to be in consideration? Many thanks Sebastian

entry.title: Withholding Tax in payment postings for Paraguay - SAP S/4 HANA Cloud
entry.content:
In Paraguay, an incoming invoice can have two line items with different VAT tax codes, one line item with 10% rate and the other with 5% rate.
 
The withholding tax is posted at payment, but the WTax Rate should be different for each tax code, for example, line item with tax code rate 10% should have WTax Rate 70% and line item with tax code rate 5% should have WTax Rate 30%.
 
This was achieved in SAP S/4 HANA by creating an account key ZST in calculation procedure and two withholding tax types for payment postings, one for VAT tax code 10% and other for VAT tax code 5%. Each WHT type is assigned to the corresponding account key in configuration activity Define Processing Key for Modified Tax Amount.  
 

This results in withholding tax info (traditionally WITH_ITEM table) with two line items, one for tax type I1 with VAT 10% and other for tax type I2 with VAT 5%, and Wtax rate can be applied for each one at payment posting.

In S/4 HANA Cloud, since configuration of the calculation procedure is not available for customer local version, is there a workaround so the W/Tax Base amount could be defined as separate line items in withholding tax information as the example above?

 
We tried to configure one W/tax type and two W/tax codes, but the same vendor can only have one W/tax type, also, the base amount would be the same for both.
 
 
And if we use account key VST for both VAT tax code with 10% and 5% rate the W/Tax Base gets duplicated.

entry.title: Re: How to Edit and Annotate an Image in SAP MDK?
entry.content:
Hi @DivanWellalage,

building a separate extension control is one option, but you could also search for an existing NativeScript plugin which offers your required functionality.

The nativescript plugin https://market.nativescript.org/plugins/nativescript-photo-editor/ for example provides crop, drawing and edit text capability for editing images. 

Robin

entry.title: Re: OOABAP
entry.content: When a program tries to create an instance of the sub class, the runtime system automatically calls the static constructors sequentially, starting at the top of the inheritance hierarchy. Once all of the static constructors have run, the runtime system calls the instance constructor of the subclass, starting at the bottom of the inheritance hierarchy. So, the sequence would be: 1.) Static Constructor of super1. 2.) Static Constructor of sub1. 3.) Instance Constructor of sub1 4.) Instance Constructor of super1

entry.title: Re: Element "timestamp" is not available in my ABAP CDS V...
entry.content: Thanks, bheemeshRao! That was the case!

entry.title: Integrating Payment Terminals with SAP Customer Checkout using the ZVT Protocol
entry.content:
Understanding ZVT Protocol

The ZVT (Zahlungsverkehrsterminals) Protocol is the standard for electronic payment transactions in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland (DACH region). Developed by the Zentraler Kreditausschuss (Central Credit Committee), this protocol has become the backbone of payment processing in retail and hospitality sectors across these regions.

Key Benefits of Using ZVT with SAP Customer Checkout:

  • Standardized Integration: ZVT provides a unified interface for various payment methods, including credit cards, debit cards, and electronic cash systems.
  • Wide Terminal Support: Compatible with popular terminal manufacturers like Ingenico and Verifone
  • Seamless Integration: Direct communication between your POS system and payment terminals
  • Real-time Processing: Quick transaction handling with immediate feedback

Installation and Setup Guide

Prerequisites

  1. SAP Customer Checkout installed
  2. A compatible ZVT-enabled payment terminal
  3. Netprint ZVT Plugin

Step 1: Obtaining the Plugin

Contact Netprint to acquire the ZVT Plugin:

  • Website: https://www.netprint.dev/zvt-plugin
  • Email: info@netprint.dev

Step 2: Plugin Installation

  1. Navigate to your SAP Customer Checkout installation directory
  2. Locate the plugins folder: C:\SapCustomerCheckout\cco\POSPlugins\AP
  3. Place the plugin files in this directory
  4. Restart SAP Customer Checkout

Step 3: Terminal Configuration

  1. Access your terminal's settings menu
  2. Ensure TCP/IP Connection Service is enabled
  3. Configure your terminal's network settings according to your payment provider's specifications
  4. Document the terminal's IP address for configuration

Step 4: SAP Customer Checkout Configuration

Essential Settings:

  1. Open SAP Customer Checkout settings
  2. Navigate to the Plugin configuration section
  3. Configure the following key parameters:
    • TERMINAL_HOST_IP: Your terminal's IP address (obtained from terminal settings)
    • TERMINAL_PORT: Configure according to your payment provider's specifications
    • TERMINAL_PASSWORD: Use the secure password provided by your payment provider
    • TERMINAL_COMPLETION_TIMEOUT: Recommended 1-2 minutes (in milliseconds)
    • CARD_TYPE_MAPPING: Maps terminal card IDs (HEX) to POS card IDs Example: 08=1;0a=2;0b=2;06=3;0c=4;05=7;2e=6;0d=8 Note: Card type IDs can be found in the ZVT specification, though terminals may return special values for certain cards. See the table for reference on how this mapping is created
    • FALLBACK_CARD_ID: e.g. 5 (“EC”) to mark cards which could not be mapped.

Card Type

Decimal

HEX

CCO

American Express

8

08

1

VISA

10

0A

2

Visa Electron

11

0B

2

Master Card

6

06

3

Diners Club (inactive)

12

0C

4

Girocard

5

05

e.g. 7

Maestro

46

2E

e.g. 6

V Pay

13

0D

e.g. 8

JCB

14

0E

 

Card Activation:

  • Enable required card types in the SAP Customer Checkout interface
  • Activate all necessary payment methods
  • Configure visibility settings as needed, it is recommended to only have one card visible with a generic name (Like “Card Payment” or “Terminal”). The ZVT Plugin will react to all card, except the ones which are in the excluded list.

Best Practices and Tips

  • Keep your terminal firmware up-to-date
  • Maintain a stable network connection
  • Regular testing of the payment process
  • Monitor transaction logs for any issues
  • Keep documentation of your configuration settings

Troubleshooting

  • Ensure proper network connectivity between POS and terminal
  • Verify correct IP address and port settings
  • Check terminal password configuration
  • Review card mapping configuration if certain cards aren't recognized

Additional Resources

For more information and support:

  • SAP Customer Checkout Documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CUSTOMER_CHECKOUT?locale=en-US
  • Netprint ZVT Plugin Documentation in PartnerEdge: https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/assets/products/cust_mng/dgl/81/87/SAP1148187.html

entry.title: Unable to consume data from a odata service
entry.content:
In my recent project, I embarked on integrating an OData service, specifically API_PRODUCT_SRV, through SAP Build. Utilizing the OData integration in the "Create Data Entity" section, I provided the base API URL and basic authentication credentials. After successful verification, I chose the "List" option under the entity and ran the test using the given username and password. The test API call response came back with a satisfying 200 OK status, returning values for all the specified fields. The schema appeared to be correct as well.

With the initial steps successfully completed, I enabled certain entities and proceeded to the UI page. Here, I dragged and dropped a list item component, created a data variable for the enabled entity, and selected the type as a collection of records. In the list item configuration, I set it to repeat with the entity data variable, adding specific fields as the primary and secondary labels.

Despite following these steps meticulously, I encountered an issue while previewing the UI: no data was displayed. Below is a summary of the steps I took:

1. OData Integration:
- Provided base API URL.
- Used basic authentication credentials.
- Verified the service.
- Chose "List" option under entity.
- Ran the test with provided username and password.
- Received 200 OK response with correct schema.

2. UI Configuration:
- Enabled entities.
- Dragged and dropped list item component.
- Created a data variable for the enabled entity (type: collection of records).
- Configured list item to repeat with entity data variable.
- Added fields as primary and secondary labels.

Issue: Data Not Displaying in Preview

I have checked the configurations multiple times and ensured that all steps were followed correctly. The API response and schema seem to be accurate, yet the data is not being rendered in the list item during the preview.

Request for Help:

If anyone has encountered a similar issue or has insights into what might be going wrong, your assistance would be greatly appreciated. Could there be a configuration step I might have overlooked, or is there a known issue with this specific OData service integration in SAP Build?

Thank you in advance for your help!


Best Regards,

Yashmin Behera

SAP Community 

SAP Build Apps 

 

@Dan_Wroblewski 

@Ivan_Miguel 

@sakshamjain

entry.title: Re: Invoicing in SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Automatic Invoicing through Contract Terms

Auto Invoicing through Contract Terms can be done in 2 different ways:

  1. Auto Invoice for Fixed & Recurring fee pricing terms
  2. ERS Invoicing for Materials & Services Pricing Term

Here, I have collated some content which gives an idea about Automatic Invoicing through Contract Terms Document. Also, both above mentioned ways are different than ERS Invoicing through Purchase Order scenario which heavily depends on certain configurations mentioned here - ERS Enablement and Configurations. In Contract Compliance we have 2 terminologies Release Order Contracts & No-Release Order Contracts. Release order contracts are nothing, but the Requisition/PO is required to create an invoice whereas for other the invoice can be created/generated without Requisition/PO against contract term.

A. Auto Invoice for Fixed & Recurring fee pricing terms

  1. Auto Invoicing through Release Order Contracts

When you create Item Level contract terms document you will have to update certain options as mentioned in the table below to enable ‘Auto Invoice’ field. Only Material Item category is supported for Auto Invoicing under Fixed & Recurring Fees Pricing term.

Only members of the User groups Invoice Manager and Contract Manager can view and edit the Auto Invoice field for Fee/Recurring Fee contract line items.

Auto Invoice schedule task considers the Contract Term only when the start date reaches, and the requisition is fully/partially received. The task will not consider a contract term for auto invoicing if the PO is not fully/partially received. Receipt should be in ‘Processed’ status if the GR is in ‘Awaiting Processing’ then the invoice won’t be generated automatically.

The Auto Invoice scheduled task runs daily and only SAP Ariba Support person can see the task & its schedule.

     2. Auto Invoicing through No-Release Order Contract

No-Release Order Contract means Invoice can be created/generated without any requisition/PO. Here in the Item-Level contract term certain options (Mentioned in below table) needs to be updated to enable Auto Invoicing. Under pricing term one can add Materials and Services Pricing Term, Fixed and Recurring Fees Pricing Term & Costs and Expenses Pricing Term. Out of these Pricing Terms only Fixed and Recurring Fees Pricing Term has option of ‘Auto Invoice’. Once the contract term document is published, scheduled task will generate an invoice based on the contract term effective date. This scheduled task runs daily.

If the supplier is ERS Enabled, then the Auto Invoice indicator will be defaulted to ‘Yes’. For Non ERS suppliers we need to set the indicator manually to ‘Yes’.

B. ERS Invoicing for Materials & Services Pricing Term

Other than Fixed and Recurring Fee pricing term the invoice automation can be performed for ‘Materials & Services’ Pricing Term. A supplier is not required to be enabled with ERS configurations for this scenario. Automatic Invoice is generated based on the scheduled task (Runs daily) once the contract receiving is completed either fully or partially.

To perform the ERS Invoicing Item Level contract term needs to be updated with certain options (Updated in the table below), once updated ‘ERS Allowed’ field will be visible under Materials & Services Pricing Term. ‘Materials & Services’ pricing term can only be configured with Non-Catalog Material item category.   

If the ERS is already enabled for a particular supplier, then ‘ERS Allowed’ field under Materials & Services Pricing Term will be defaulted to ‘Yes’ else ‘No’.

Pre-requisites:

 Use Cases –

  • In the case of recurring payments (e.g., subscription services, ongoing supply contracts), Ariba can automatically generate invoices at regular intervals (monthly, quarterly, etc.) with the help of Fixed & Recurring Fee Pricing Term. In case requisitions are required then Release Order Contract can be leveraged.
  • For contracts that involve delivering a specific quantity of goods over time (e.g., phased deliveries), ERS Allowed field (Materials & Services Pricing Term) can be used to automatically generate invoices as goods are received. This is useful for contracts with clear delivery schedules and agreed-upon quantities.

References –

  • No Release Order Contract
  • ERS Enablement and Configurations

entry.title: Re: Substitutions for Market segment using g/l account in...

entry.title: Re: Celebrating 2 Years of the Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano ,

I have completed my learning journey and sent the confirmation via e-mail on Dec 1st. Since then, I haven't received any communication from SAP, including the redemption code. Is there anything I missed, are you still processing submissions?

Best regards,

Natalia
